# signoz_auth_domain (Resource)

Creates and manages auth domains in SigNoz. An auth domain pins a single SSO provider (Google OAuth, OIDC, or SAML) for users belonging to a given email domain.

The provider config is a one-of: **exactly one** of `google_auth_config`, `oidc_config`, or `saml_config` must be set inside the `config` block. Setting more than one — or none — fails plan with an `ExactlyOneNestedAttribute` validator error.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    signoz = {
      source = "registry.terraform.io/signoz/signoz"
    }
  }
}

provider "signoz" {
  endpoint     = "http://localhost:8080"
  access_token = "<SIGNOZ-API-KEY>"
}

# Google OAuth — exactly one of google_auth_config / oidc_config /
# saml_config must be set inside `config`.
resource "signoz_auth_domain" "google" {
  name = "example.com"
  config = {
    sso_type = "google_auth"
    google_auth_config = {
      client_id     = "9999999999-example.apps.googleusercontent.com"
      client_secret = "GOOGLE-CLIENT-SECRET"
    }
  }
}

# OIDC — same shape, different nested config.
resource "signoz_auth_domain" "oidc" {
  name = "example.org"
  config = {
    sso_type = "oidc"
    oidc_config = {
      issuer        = "https://idp.example.org"
      client_id     = "signoz"
      client_secret = "OIDC-CLIENT-SECRET"
    }
  }
}

output "auth_domain_id" {
  value = signoz_auth_domain.google.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->

## Schema

### Optional

- `config` (Attributes) Writable SSO configuration. (see below for nested schema)
- `name` (String) Email domain (e.g., `example.com`). The server may also derive this from the org context.

### Read-Only

- `auth_nprovider_info` (Attributes) Server-side metadata about the auth provider, including SAML relay-state path. (see below for nested schema)
- `google_auth_config` (Attributes) Read-back view of the active Google OAuth config (mirrors `config.google_auth_config`). (see below for nested schema)
- `id` (String) Autogenerated unique ID.
- `oidc_config` (Attributes) Read-back view of the active OIDC config. (see below for nested schema)
- `org_id` (String) ID of the org this auth domain belongs to.
- `role_mapping` (Attributes) Read-back view of the active role mapping. (see below for nested schema)
- `saml_config` (Attributes) Read-back view of the active SAML config. (see below for nested schema)
- `sso_enabled` (Boolean) Whether SSO is currently enabled for this domain.
- `sso_type` (String) Currently selected SSO type.

### Nested Schema for `config`

Optional:

- `google_auth_config` (Attributes) Google OAuth config. (see below for nested schema)
- `oidc_config` (Attributes) OIDC config. (see below for nested schema)
- `role_mapping` (Attributes) Role mapping rules. (see below for nested schema)
- `saml_config` (Attributes) SAML config. (see below for nested schema)
- `sso_enabled` (Boolean) Toggle SSO on/off without removing the config.
- `sso_type` (String) Selected SSO type. One of: `google_auth`, `saml`, `email_password`, `oidc`.

Validation: exactly one of `google_auth_config`, `oidc_config`, `saml_config` must be set.

### Nested Schema for `config.google_auth_config`

Optional:

- `allowed_groups` (List of String) Workspace groups whose members may sign in.
- `client_id` (String) OAuth client ID.
- `client_secret` (String, Sensitive) OAuth client secret.
- `domain_to_admin_email` (Map of String) Map of domain → admin email used for group lookups.
- `fetch_groups` (Boolean) Whether to fetch the user's group memberships at login.
- `fetch_transitive_group_membership` (Boolean) Whether group membership is resolved transitively.
- `insecure_skip_email_verified` (Boolean) Skip the `email_verified` check on the OAuth token.
- `redirect_uri` (String) Override the OAuth redirect URI.
- `service_account_json` (String, Sensitive) Service-account JSON used for Directory API group lookups.

### Nested Schema for `config.oidc_config`

Optional:

- `claim_mapping` (Attributes) Map OIDC claims to SigNoz user fields. (see below for nested schema)
- `client_id` (String) OIDC client ID.
- `client_secret` (String, Sensitive) OIDC client secret.
- `get_user_info` (Boolean) Whether to call the userinfo endpoint after token exchange.
- `insecure_skip_email_verified` (Boolean) Skip the `email_verified` check.
- `issuer` (String) OIDC issuer URL.
- `issuer_alias` (String) Alternate issuer URL accepted in tokens.

### Nested Schema for `config.oidc_config.claim_mapping`

Optional:

- `email` (String) Claim that carries the user's email.
- `groups` (String) Claim that carries the user's groups.
- `name` (String) Claim that carries the user's display name.
- `role` (String) Claim that carries the user's role.

### Nested Schema for `config.saml_config`

Optional:

- `attribute_mapping` (Attributes) Map SAML attributes to SigNoz user fields. (see below for nested schema)
- `insecure_skip_auth_nrequests_signed` (Boolean) Skip signing AuthN requests.
- `saml_cert` (String) IdP certificate (PEM).
- `saml_entity` (String) IdP entity ID.
- `saml_idp` (String) IdP SSO URL.

### Nested Schema for `config.saml_config.attribute_mapping`

Optional:

- `email` (String), `groups` (String), `name` (String), `role` (String) — same fields as the OIDC `claim_mapping`.

### Nested Schema for `config.role_mapping`

Optional:

- `default_role` (String) Role assigned when no group matches.
- `group_mappings` (Map of String) Map of group name → SigNoz role.
- `use_role_attribute` (Boolean) Whether to read the role from a SAML/OIDC attribute instead of `group_mappings`.
